name: Caradoc
description: Caradoc, a prominent figure in Welsh and British mythology, appears in various tales, most notably within the Mabinogion and Arthurian legends. Often associated with courage, loyalty, and unwavering virtue, Caradoc's lineage and specific deeds vary across different texts. He is frequently depicted as a knight of the Round Table, renowned for his chivalry and prowess in battle. In some accounts, he is the son of King Llyr Marini and plays a crucial role in defending his kingdom against invaders. His character is marked by a strong sense of justice and a willingness to sacrifice himself for the greater good. Caradoc's presence in Arthurian romances underscores the ideals of knighthood and the importance of moral integrity in a tumultuous era. He is sometimes portrayed as having supernatural abilities or connections to the Otherworld, further solidifying his status as a heroic figure deeply embedded within the rich tapestry of British folklore. Caradoc serves as a symbol of resilience and unwavering commitment to upholding righteousness in the face of adversity, making him a compelling character in the Arthurian cycle.
subtitle: Valiant Knight of the Round Table
companions: Other Knights of the Round Table

keyMyths.title: Caradoc's Loyalty
keyMyths.description: Caradoc is known for his unwavering loyalty to King Arthur and his fellow knights. This loyalty often leads him into dangerous situations where he risks his life for the sake of the kingdom.

keyMyths.title: Caradoc's Bravery in Battle
keyMyths.description: Caradoc is often depicted as a fearless warrior who excels in combat. His bravery and skill in battle are crucial in defending the realm from various threats.

keyMyths.title: Caradoc and the Supernatural
keyMyths.description: Some tales associate Caradoc with supernatural elements, suggesting he possesses unique abilities or a connection to the Otherworld. This connection adds to his mystique and heroic stature.

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian legends, including mentions of Caradoc.
